reasoning: The application visualizes agricultural sales territory data (Feed Mills, Hog Producers, Grain, Growers). A light, earthy theme establishes trust and aligns with the natural/agricultural domain, while maintaining the structural clarity necessary for a data-dense control room dashboard.

layout.description: Dense, full-height application layout. A rigid grid dividing controls and visualization.

structure.container: h-screen w-full flex flex-col md:flex-row bg-stone-100 overflow-hidden
structure.sidebar: w-full md:w-80 lg:w-96 flex-shrink-0 border-r border-stone-200 bg-white h-full flex flex-col z-10 shadow-sm
structure.main_area: flex-grow relative h-full bg-stone-50 flex flex-col

layout.spacing: Tight spacing for widgets (p-4 to p-6). Every pixel utilized.
layout.surface_strategy: Grid Borders (The 'Technical' Look). Use flat, crisp containers with 1px border-stone-200. Avoid soft shadows. Use sharp 4px or 8px border-radius maximum.

components.buttons: Shadcn buttons. Flat, subtle hover states.
components.layer_toggles: Shadcn Switch or Checkbox components aligned right in list items.
components.panels: Shadcn ScrollArea for the sidebar content. Shadcn Accordion for grouping City vs County layers.
components.tooltips: Shadcn Tooltip or custom absolute positioned div tied to cursor coordinates for map hover states.

visual_enhancers.map_styles: Base map color: #E7E5E4. Stroke: #FFFFFF. Choropleth uses d3-scale to map data intensities to the opacity/shade of the county layer color. City layers use distinct colored circle markers with mix-blend-mode: multiply to highlight overlap regions.
visual_enhancers.empty_state: When no data is uploaded, display an empty state covering the map area. Use the provided aerial landscape image as a highly faded background (opacity 10%) with an upload CTA in the center.

image_urls.description: Minimal aerial view of a farm/trees. Use as a blurred, low-opacity background behind the initial file upload dropzone.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install required dependencies: yarn add react-simple-maps topojson-client d3-scale papaparse xlsx @phosphor-icons/react lucide-react
Use papaparse to parse uploaded CSVs, and xlsx to parse XLSX files. Handle the file drop locally.
For the US map, use a standard US TopoJSON URL (e.g., https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/us-atlas@3/counties-10m.json) with react-simple-maps.
Implement the map such that County data affects the `fill` color of `<Geography>` paths (using d3-scale for color intensity based on counts).
Implement the City data as `<Marker>` components over the map, scaling radius by count size or utilizing distinct colors per layer.
Apply `mix-blend-mode: multiply` on marker dots so overlap zones naturally darken/highlight 'win zones'.
Ensure all toggle switches and layer states are managed centrally, and the map recalculates and re-renders dynamically.
Include a 'Top Zones' or 'Analytics' card in the sidebar that identifies which states or counties have the highest combined opportunity based on active layers.
Add 'data-testid' attributes to all checkboxes, file inputs, and major UI components.
